summary: Set 072 Session 2 of 4 -- the external-target wrapper + per-cell telemetry + report artifact. Ran on claude-opus-4-8/high. DELIVERED (all additive, REUSES run_dual_surface matrix mode + merge_findings; NO new arm-execution logic): the net-new ai_router/verification_only_app.py -- (1) MatrixRow/MatrixCell + pair_matrix_rows (push x pull cross-product of surface rows; requires >=1 of each surface) + parse_cell_specs (--cell SURFACE:PROVIDER[:MODEL]); (2) run_verification_matrix: ONE matrix-mode run_dual_surface call per cell, pointed at the EXTERNAL target via the runner's sandbox_dir seam (and session_set_dir=target so build_instruction degrades gracefully on a repo with no spec.md), with the cell's per-arm push/pull providers/models -- so every cell is matrix mode (a per-cell instrument, never RETIRE telemetry by construction). A per-cell failure is recorded as a SkippedCell and the matrix continues (L-067-1 producer-skip). The committed diff is shared by all cells, so it is measured ONCE via the runner's own _dispatch_get_diff (DiffShape: bytes/lines/files/elided) and stamped into every cell. (3) CellTelemetry stamps every confound -- orchestrator provider/model (held constant, recorded for later comparability), push & pull provider/model + per-arm framing strength (READ FROM the run attestation, not re-asserted), surfaces run, diff size/shape, and push_broker/pull_broker='none' (a future track, stamped literally). (4) VerificationOnlyReport -> verification-matrix-report.json + a pure-Python validate_matrix_report mirroring validate_comparison_artifact (L-066-1, never raises; reuses dual_surface_verify._validate_merged_finding for the cell findings' provenance invariants). (5) build_remediation_report: consolidates EVERY cell's raw push+pull findings via merge_findings ONCE across the whole run (provenance push-only/pull-only/both; keyed defects dedup across cells; unkeyed stay safely over-split), severity-ranks (Critical>Major>Minor>unspecified), drops all experiment metadata (telemetry/provider/framing/diff) -- the fixer-facing artifact a target remediates from WITHOUT re-running verification -- + validate_remediation_report (L-066-1) + render_remediation_markdown (ASCII) + write_reports (json + md, utf-8). (6) CLI `run` subcommand (ASCII status, returns int) writing BOTH reports; injection seams (run_dual_surface_fn / diff_dispatch_fn) resolved from module globals at call-time so the CLI is monkeypatch-testable. Plus docs/verification-matrix-report-schema.md + docs/remediation-report-schema.md (orchestrator-facing references; validator-wins parity, two-outputs-one-run + consumer-handoff + matrix-is-not-RETIRE-evidence framing) and a COMMENTARY-ONLY verification_only block under pull_verifier in router-config.yaml (no behavioral knob, nothing reads it; documents the shipped best-guess matrix defaults incl the Gemini-pull-strong-framing experiment cell; live default pull provider UNCHANGED). TESTS (+20, all hermetic injected fakes, NO metered calls): pair_matrix_rows cross-product + both-surfaces guard + parse_cell_specs bad-spec; per-cell telemetry stamps every confound; external sandbox_dir honored (the fake asserts it received the external path positionally AND as sandbox_dir); validate_matrix_report accepts well-formed + round-trips JSON + expected-target guard, rejects non-object / bad-schema (bool-not-int) / bad-cell (telemetry int-as-bool, missing field) / unexpected-top-key / non-both-surfaces / cell-vs-run-level confound drift / cell provenance inconsistency; build_remediation_report consolidates both-provenance (a keyed D1 caught on push in one cell + pull in another -> ONE 'both', max-severity Critical) + severity-ranks + unkeyed-stay-split + round-trips; CLI writes both reports under a monkeypatched fake runner + ASCII .md + bad-matrix nonzero. SESSION VERIFICATION: cross-provider, routed_gate REQUIRED (blast-radius/multi-module/breadth/build-ci-config; 5 files, modules {ai_router, docs}); routed session-verification to gpt-5-4 (cross-provider from the anthropic orchestrator). R1 ISSUES_FOUND, 2 Major validator-parity gaps (~$0.229): (1) validate_matrix_report too loose -- surfaces accepted any non-empty subset + no cell-vs-run-level confound consistency check; (2) _validate_cell looser than its validate_comparison_artifact sibling on the provenanceComplete<->unkeyed invariant. FIXED Finding 1 in full (surfaces EXACTLY [push,pull] rejecting missing/duplicate; validate_matrix_report enforces each cell's echoed orchestrator*/diff* == run-level orchestrator/diffShape, the Set-070 cross-field-invariant precedent; schema doc updated) + the legitimate part of Finding 2 (_validate_cell now applies the SAME one-way provenanceComplete=>no-unkeyed + zero-counts checks as the sibling). DECLINED the Finding-2 over-reach (the converse + exact count recompute) with reasoning: the spec instruction is to MIRROR validate_comparison_artifact (the canonical sibling deliberately enforces only the one-way checks), a false provenanceComplete is conservative not corrupting, and the producer derives flag+counts from one merge_findings result so cannot emit an inconsistency. +3 tests. R2 VERIFIED (~$0.130): both fixes confirmed + the decline judged sound (no plausible path to harm exceeding what the sibling tolerates). s2-verification.md (R1) + s2-verification-r2.md (R2) saved raw, never edited. SUITE: full ai_router GREEN 2205 passed / 5 skipped (-m 'not e2e'; the 5 are the Set 069 S4 real-Podman-on-Windows by-design skips; +20 from this session). CONCURRENCY NOTE: a separate chat session edited shared writers (session_log.py, contract_gate.py, dedicated_verification.py, dual_surface_verify.py, path_aware_critique.py, suggestion_disposition.py) + the repo-wide activity-log.json files mid-run (removing the empty routedApiCalls key). This explains a transient test_change_verification_mode KeyError on the FIRST full-suite pass (passed in isolation AND on the deterministic re-run -- 2205/5 with 0 failures; that module is untouched by this session). My staging is SURGICAL (explicit paths only, never `git add -A`); the other session's in-flight files are left entirely to it. NEXT S3 routed rec (route analysis -> s2-next-session.md, per L-064-6): a cost-conscious mid-tier model at medium effort, non-anthropic preferred for fresh eyes; the analysis model returned STALE ids (gpt-4o / claude-3-sonnet) mapped to this repo's roster as claude/claude-sonnet-4-6/medium (primary, repo-valid mid-tier) with openai/gpt-5-4/medium the non-anthropic fresh-eyes alternative, for the cross-run remediation aggregator (aggregate_remediation_reports + remediation-backlog.{json,md} + validate_remediation_backlog + CLI aggregate). The operator chooses.

reason.specifics: Routed analysis (route task_type=analysis -> s2-next-session.md, per L-064-6) recommends a cost-conscious mid-tier model at medium effort, non-anthropic preferred for fresh eyes, for Session 3 (the cross-run remediation aggregator: aggregate_remediation_reports + remediation-backlog.{json,md} + validate_remediation_backlog + CLI aggregate -- a focused extension of the S2 module reusing the merge_findings provenance machinery, prioritizing produce<->validate parity over novel reasoning). The analysis model emitted STALE model ids (gpt-4o / claude-3-sonnet-20240229); mapped to this repo's actual roster that is claude/claude-sonnet-4-6/medium as the concrete repo-valid mid-tier primary (matching the S1 pattern), with openai/gpt-5-4/medium the non-anthropic fresh-eyes alternative the routed analysis actually preferred. The operator chooses; a non-anthropic engine is defensible for independent eyes on the validator-parity surface.
